mcqs.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of primitive data structures?

options.text: User-defined
Predefined by the programming language
Variable size
Complex

mcqs.explanation: Primitive data structures are predefined by the programming language, meaning they are built-in and do not require user definition.

mcqs.question: What is the primary difference between linear and non-linear data structures?

options.text: Linear data structures are user-defined, while non-linear data structures are predefined.
Linear data structures have a fixed size, while non-linear data structures have a variable size.
Linear data structures arrange elements in a sequential manner, while non-linear data structures do not.
Linear data structures are more complex than non-linear data structures.

mcqs.explanation: Linear data structures, such as arrays and linked lists, arrange elements in a sequential manner. Non-linear data structures, such as trees and graphs, do not arrange elements in a sequential manner.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of a non-primitive data structure?

options.text: Integer
Array
Boolean
Float

mcqs.explanation: Arrays are an example of a non-primitive data structure, as they are derived from primitive data structures and can store multiple values.

mcqs.question: What is the primary advantage of using a stack data structure?

options.text: Efficient insertion and deletion of elements at any position.
Efficient searching of elements.
Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) access, which is useful for parsing and evaluating expressions.
First-In-First-Out (FIFO) access, which is useful for job scheduling.

mcqs.explanation: Stacks are particularly useful for parsing and evaluating expressions, as they follow the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a tree data structure?

options.text: Elements are arranged in a sequential manner.
Each element has a fixed number of children.
Elements are connected by edges, and each element has a parent-child relationship.
Elements are stored in a contiguous block of memory.

mcqs.explanation: Trees are characterized by elements connected by edges, with each element having a parent-child relationship.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of a graph data structure?

options.text: A social network
A file system
A database
A compiler

mcqs.explanation: A social network is an example of a graph data structure, as it consists of nodes (people) connected by edges (friendships).

mcqs.question: What is the primary difference between a queue and a stack?

options.text: A queue follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle, while a stack follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle.
A queue follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle, while a stack follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle.
A queue is used for parsing expressions, while a stack is used for job scheduling.
A queue is used for job scheduling, while a stack is used for parsing expressions.

mcqs.explanation: A queue follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle, while a stack follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle.
